questions.question: An a.c. generator is connected to an oscilloscope. The trace shows a peak voltage of $2\text{ V}$ and a period of $0.04\text{ s}$. What is the frequency of the a.c. signal?
options: $25\text{ Hz}$
$50\text{ Hz}$
$0.04\text{ Hz}$
$2\text{ Hz}$
questions.explanation:
Frequency $f = 1/T = 1 / 0.04 = 25\text{ Hz}$. 

**Common mistake:** Using the voltage value ($2\text{ V}$) in the frequency calculation.

questions.question: Which of the following procedures does NOT generate an induced e.m.f. in a coil of wire?
options: Holding a magnet stationary inside the coil
Rotating the coil in a magnetic field
Rotating a magnet around the stationary coil
Moving a bar magnet towards the coil
questions.explanation:
Based on Review Question 6, holding a magnet stationary results in no change in magnetic flux, so no e.m.f. is induced. 

**Common mistake:** Thinking being 'inside' a magnetic field is enough, regardless of motion.

questions.question: In electromagnetic induction, which of the following factors does NOT affect the magnitude of the induced e.m.f.?
options: The resistance of the coil
The strength of the magnetic field
The speed of the coil's motion
The number of turns in the coil
questions.explanation:
Directly from Review Question 7. Resistance affects the current ($I = V/R$), but the induced e.m.f. ($V$) depends only on the rate of cutting field lines. 

**Common mistake:** Confusing e.m.f. (voltage) with the resulting current.

questions.question: The diagram shows a graph of induced e.m.f. against time for a generator. Which change was made to the generator to produce the second curve?nn[Scenario: Curve 2 has half the period and twice the peak voltage of Curve 1]
options: The frequency of rotation was doubled.
The number of turns in the coil was doubled.
The magnetic field strength was doubled.
The resistance of the external circuit was halved.
questions.explanation:
According to Worked Example 18B and Figure 18.11, doubling the frequency of rotation ($f$) results in both doubling the peak voltage and halving the period ($T = 1/f$). 

**Common mistake:** Choosing 'doubling turns', which would increase voltage but not change the period.

questions.question: Which row in the table correctly describes the function of slip rings in an a.c. generator?
options: They provide a continuous connection between the rotating coil and the fixed brushes.
They reverse the current direction every half-turn of the coil.
They convert the induced a.c. into a d.c. output.
They increase the magnetic field strength of the permanent magnets.
questions.explanation:
Slip rings maintain electrical contact with the brushes while the coil rotates, without twisting the wires. 

**Common mistake:** Attributing the current-reversal function to slip rings; that is the role of a commutator in a motor.
